sketches.mechanism: The `object` declaration creates a singleton — exactly one instance can ever exist, and it is created lazily upon first access.
sketches.resonance: Loneliness is not an error or an edge case but a language-enforced guarantee: there will only ever be one.

sketches.mechanism: Prolog's query resolution: when no facts satisfy a query, it returns `false` — not as an error, but as a logical conclusion from absence of evidence.
sketches.resonance: Asking whether you were loved and receiving `false` — not because someone denied it, but because no evidence was ever recorded.

sketches.mechanism: Go channels block on receive when empty; a goroutine waiting on a channel that no one ever sends to will wait forever, causing a fatal `deadlock` at runtime.
sketches.resonance: Waiting for a message that was never sent is not patience — the runtime eventually names it what it is.
